# HSTU representative baseline environment boundary

Upstream commit:
`6135bc30398f97e5786674192558d91f2ef2fa90`

The official repository reports Ubuntu 22.04, CUDA 12.4, and Python 3.10, and
declares the following lower bounds:

```text
torch>=2.6.0
fbgemm_gpu>=1.1.0
torchrec>=1.1.0
gin_config>=0.5.0
pandas>=2.2.0
tensorboard>=2.19.0
pybind11
```

Use a dedicated Python 3.10 environment. The active project environment is
Python 3.13 and does not currently contain fbgemm-gpu, torchrec, or gin-config;
it must not be silently modified or treated as a successful HSTU environment.

The first scientific smoke test uses the official research HSTU implementation
and PyTorch kernel path. The CUTLASS submodule and optimized CUDA/Triton kernels
are efficiency-only and are not prerequisites for the motivation experiment.

An existing isolated environment was audited on 2026-07-15 as:

```text
conda_env=hstu
python==3.10.20
torch==2.6.0+cu124
fbgemm_gpu==1.1.0
torchrec==1.1.0
triton==3.2.0
pandas==2.2.0
```

In this fbgemm build, importing `fbgemm_gpu` alone did not register
`asynchronous_complete_cumsum`; importing TorchRec did register it in the audited
run. The official-core smoke script verifies the operator and loads the packaged
`fbgemm_gpu_sparse_async_cumsum.so` only if it is still missing. No vendored HSTU
source is patched.
